source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/09_HnVUE/MFDS/참고자료/03. 제품의 성능 및 안전을 확인하기 위한 자료/03. 성능에 관한 자료/01. 소프트웨어 적합성/4.2 [HnVUE] Software Development Plan (A-SDP-HNV)_.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software Development Plan for HnVUE, a standalone medical device software for medical image acquisition, processing, storage, transmission, and display in diagnostic radiography.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software development planning, software classification, requirements, risk management, verification and validation, release, and configuration management documentation for HnVUE.
keyPoints: HnVUE is described as standalone medical device software and a Medical Image Management and Processing System.
The software interfaces with digital flat panel X-ray detectors for diagnostic radiography.
The plan references FDA Level of Concern classification and EN 62304 software safety classes.
The development process follows a classic waterfall methodology.
The plan includes requirements for DICOM, PACS worklist, image processing, viewer tools, settings, logging, security, and detector information.
Software release requires completed verification, evaluated results, residual abnormality documentation, release version documentation, and controlled distribution procedures.

evidence.claim: FDA Level of Concern categories are Major, Moderate, and Minor.
evidence.evidenceText: S/W is classified into Major, Moderate and Minor by its level of concern

evidence.claim: The document includes EN 62304 software safety classification.
evidence.evidenceText: Software classification in accordance with EN 62304

evidence.claim: EN 62304 safety classes are described as Class A, Class B, and Class C.
evidence.evidenceText: Class A: There is no injury nor health damage. -. Class B: The possible harm is non-serious injury. -. Class C: The possible harm is death or serious injury.

evidence.claim: The development process follows classic waterfall methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process of H&abyz Co., Ltd. follows “Classic waterfall methodology"

evidence.claim: The development schedule includes analysis, planning, design, implementation, qualification, maintenance, release, and development plan update steps.
evidence.evidenceText: Step Main Contents Development Period Analysis ... Planning ... Design ... Implementation ... Qualification ... Maintenance ... Software release ... Development plan updated

evidence.claim: Software development resources include Windows 10 64bit, .NET Framework 4.8, .NET Framework 8, C# Microsoft Visual Studio 2022, C++ Microsoft Visual Studio 2019(v142), and SQLite 3.46.1.
evidence.evidenceText: Windows 10 64bit .NET Framework 4.8 .NET Framework 8 C# Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 C++ Microsoft Visual Studio 2019(v142) SQLite 3.46.1

evidence.claim: HnVUE supports DICOM image standards and DICOM export.
evidence.evidenceText: SRS 100 DICOM standard Image Standard Supports medical image standards. ... SRS 101 DICOM standard DICOM Export

evidence.claim: DICOM image files are transferred to a PACS storage server.
evidence.evidenceText: DICOM image files are transferred to the PACS storage server.

evidence.claim: HnVUE can acquire images from the detector via Ethernet communication.
evidence.evidenceText: Acquire images captured from the detector via Ethernet communication.

evidence.claim: HnVUE supports multi-detector operation up to 3 detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: Multi-detector support - up to 3 detectors

evidence.claim: HnVUE includes image stitching functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: The captured images of the patient are stitchable. Chapters and two or three images can be stitched

evidence.claim: HnVUE supports PACS worklist patient registration and manual order registration.
evidence.evidenceText: You can check the capture order on the PACS worklist server or manually register the order by the user.

evidence.claim: The plan includes audit log functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: The administrator can check what the user did, when, and who it is.

evidence.claim: The plan includes DCM header encryption and DB encryption requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Encryption of security-required header information for all DCM files ... Encrypt the DB so that only those with a password can access it.

evidence.claim: Password length is tied to HIPAA regulations in the SRS.
evidence.evidenceText: Password must be at least 8 characters long. (HIPAA regulations)

evidence.claim: Software risk analysis references EN ISO14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Software risk analysis Identification of the characteristics and hazards associated with the intended use and the safety of the device, risk assessment EN ISO14971:2019

evidence.claim: Software development planning references EN ISO13485:2016.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development Devise a plan for the software development and put it in writing EN ISO13485:2016

evidence.claim: Software verification and validation includes original code test, unit test, integration test, and system test.
evidence.evidenceText: Test item Original code test Unit test Integration test System test

evidence.claim: Software release requires verification completion and evaluation before release.
evidence.evidenceText: It should be ensured that the software verification is completed and that the results are evaluated before releasing the software.

evidence.claim: Configuration versions use the format Version.X.Y.Z.
evidence.evidenceText: it should be able to distinguish by a version name and write as the following type. * Version. X.Y.Z

evidence.claim: Software configuration changes should be validated before application.
evidence.evidenceText: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be validated before it is applied to the software.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present; signature table date fields and revision date are blank.
Manufacturer name in the purpose section is redacted as blank underscores.
Authority is ambiguous: source metadata indicates MFDS/domestic, while the document text explicitly references FDA guidelines and international standards but does not mention MFDS.
Several SRS table rows appear misaligned or terse in the preprocessed text, so detailed row-level interpretation should be reviewed against the original document.
